name: why-we-get-fat
description: Gary Taubes's Why We Get Fat: And What to Do About It — a nutritional science toolkit that challenges the conventional 'calories in, calories out' model and presents the carbohydrate-insulin hypothesis: that hormonal regulation (especially insulin), not caloric balance, controls body fat — and what to do about it.
useCase: Understand the science behind the carbohydrate-insulin hypothesis of obesity, critique conventional nutrition advice, and implement a low-carb lifestyle that works with your body's hormonal system rather than against it.
knownRisks: The carbohydrate-insulin hypothesis is still debated in the medical and nutritional communities
Low-carb diets may not be appropriate for all individuals, including those with certain medical conditions
The book's strong critique of established nutrition science may be unsettling for some readers
